skill.name: prometheus-configuration
skill.description: Configure Prometheus metric collection, scrape targets, recording rules, alert rules, and validation workflows for infrastructure and applications.
skill.summary: Configure Prometheus monitoring for metrics, scraping, rules, alerts, and validation.
skill.author: wshobson

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many command, network, certificate, weak-crypto, and heuristic findings in SKILL.md. Review found no malicious intent or prompt injection; the risky content is Markdown guidance for Prometheus setup, including commands that can install charts or query endpoints. Publish with a medium warning because agent execution of the operational commands could change infrastructure.

medium_findings.title: Operational Commands Can Modify Monitoring Infrastructure
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE with legitimate context. SKILL.md includes Helm, promtool, and curl command examples for installing Prometheus and validating targets. The commands are not hidden or malicious, but an autonomous agent could run them and change a Kubernetes namespace or local monitoring setup.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are directly visible in Markdown code blocks and include helm install and validation commands. The context is operational documentation, so this is a controlled medium risk rather than malicious execution.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE for malicious network behavior. The external URL is the public Prometheus Community Helm chart repository, and the remaining URLs point to localhost Prometheus API endpoints for validation. No credential transmission or external exfiltration endpoint was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are shown in setup and troubleshooting examples. Their purpose is clear from nearby headings and there is no evidence of data exfiltration.

low_findings.title: TLS Key File References Do Not Include Secret Material
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE for sensitive data exposure. SKILL.md references ca_file, cert_file, and key_file paths in a Prometheus TLS configuration example, but it does not embed certificates, private keys, passwords, or tokens.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines contain file paths only, not credential contents. This is a common Prometheus configuration pattern.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged lines contain skill metadata, alert descriptions, and threshold text. No cryptographic API, algorithm selection, hashing code, or encryption workflow was found at those locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of each cited line found only prose or Prometheus alert text. There is no semantic evidence of weak cryptographic use.

low_findings.title: Critical Combination Heuristic Dismissed After Context Review
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE for malicious behavior. The scanner combined command examples, localhost network examples, and TLS file path examples into a critical heuristic. Manual review found no obfuscation, no prompt injection, no hidden execution flow, and no credential exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The underlying patterns are real but appear in transparent documentation examples. No file content attempts to override instructions or conceal behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Commands in Markdown Guidance
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes bash examples that install a Helm chart, query Prometheus, and inspect local endpoints. These are expected for a Prometheus configuration skill, but agents should request confirmation before executing them.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The shell snippets are explicit and relevant to the skill domain. The risk depends on whether an agent executes them without user approval.

content.value_statement: Teams need reliable Prometheus setups that collect useful metrics without noisy rules or broken scrape jobs. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through scrape configuration, recording rules, alert rules, and validation steps.
actual_capabilities: Design Prometheus scrape configurations for static targets, files, and Kubernetes service discovery.
Create recording rules for request rates, error rates, latency, CPU, memory, and disk metrics.
Draft alert rules for availability, latency, error rate, and resource usage signals.
Explain Helm and Docker Compose setup patterns for Prometheus deployments.
Provide validation steps using promtool and Prometheus HTTP API checks.
limitations: It does not discover live infrastructure unless the user provides environment details.
It cannot verify metric names without access to real Prometheus targets or exports.
It provides examples that must be adapted before production use.
It does not replace security review for TLS keys, network exposure, or access controls.

use_cases.title: Bootstrap Kubernetes Monitoring
use_cases.description: Plan a Prometheus deployment with Helm settings, retention, storage, and Kubernetes scrape discovery.

use_cases.title: Standardize Service Metrics
use_cases.description: Create scrape jobs and labels for application teams that expose metrics endpoints.

use_cases.title: Improve Alert Quality
use_cases.description: Build recording rules and alerts that focus on service health, latency, errors, and resource pressure.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Prometheus Setup
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for one service or host group.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Prometheus configuration for my service. Include scrape interval, target labels, and a simple validation checklist.

prompt_templates.title: Add Kubernetes Service Discovery
prompt_templates.scenario: Use Kubernetes annotations and relabeling for dynamic targets.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design Prometheus Kubernetes service discovery for annotated pods and services. Explain each relabeling rule in simple terms.

prompt_templates.title: Build Recording and Alert Rules
prompt_templates.scenario: Create reusable metrics and alert thresholds for production services.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create recording rules and alert rules for request rate, error rate, P95 latency, CPU, memory, and disk usage.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Production Configuration
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of an existing Prometheus design.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Prometheus configuration for scrape reliability, label quality, alert noise, retention, and validation gaps. Recommend specific fixes.

output_examples.input: I need Prometheus monitoring for three application servers and node exporters.
output_examples.output: A structured plan with scrape jobs, target labels, retention settings, and validation steps for target health.

output_examples.input: Our alerts fire too often during short spikes.
output_examples.output: Recommendations for recording rules, longer alert windows, severity labels, and thresholds that reduce noise.

output_examples.input: We want Kubernetes pods to opt in to scraping by annotation.
output_examples.output: A service discovery design with annotation filters, metric path handling, port mapping, and namespace labels.

best_practices: Confirm target names, ports, and metric paths before applying generated scrape configurations.
Validate every configuration and rule file with promtool before deployment.
Review alert thresholds with service owners before enabling paging alerts.
anti_patterns: Do not run Helm or curl commands from generated guidance without checking the target cluster or host.
Do not expose Prometheus endpoints publicly without authentication, TLS, and network controls.
Do not copy example TLS key paths without planning secure file ownership and permissions.

faq.question: Can this skill install Prometheus for me?
faq.answer: It can provide setup commands and configuration guidance. You should approve and run infrastructure commands only after review.

faq.question: Does it support Kubernetes monitoring?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes Helm setup guidance and Kubernetes service discovery patterns for pods and services.

faq.question: Can it create alert rules?
faq.answer: Yes. It can draft alert rules for downtime, error rate, latency, CPU, memory, and disk usage.

faq.question: Can it validate my Prometheus files?
faq.answer: It can suggest promtool checks and review configuration structure. Actual validation requires running tools in your environment.

faq.question: Does it include Grafana dashboards?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on Prometheus configuration, scrape jobs, recording rules, alert rules, and validation.

faq.question: Is it safe for production use?
faq.answer: Use it as implementation guidance. Review generated commands, TLS paths, labels, retention, and alert thresholds before production deployment.
